sections.paragraph:
δουλαγωγέω (Diod S 17, 70, 6; Epict. 3, 24, 76; 4, 7, 17; Just., A II, 11, 7, D. 110, 4; Ath., R. 72, 6) enslave, subjugate fig. (Chariton 2, 7, 1; Περὶ ὕψους 44, 6; Theophyl. Sim., Ep. 36; Herm. Wr. Fgm. XXIII, 48 p. 484, 13 Sc.; Procop., Hist., Aed. 1, 9, 4) the body (w. ὑπωπιάζω) Paul makes a slave out of his body, i.e. he directs it for useful service: I make it obey me (New Life) 1 Cor 9:27.—S. DELG s.v. ἄγω p. 18 and δοῦλο. M-M. TW.

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Chariton Chariton , scriptor eroticus (writer of romances) I–II a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

New New Life = The New Life Testament—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 259
